He was responsible for managing large and diverse teams and building cutting edge technology in complex, multidisciplinary environments.

speaker_job_title: SVP, Acquiring Partnerships, Product, and Strategy for Merchant Services - US
speaker_description: Antonio Gagliardi is Senior Vice President of Acquiring Partnerships, Product, and Strategy for U.S. Merchant Services at American Express.  He is responsible for growing card acceptance with traditional and fintech Partners.Prior to this role, Antonio oversaw Strategy and M&A for Global Commercial Services at AmEx. Prior, Antonio launched and scaled a VC-backed financial comparison platform in Europe. He started his career at McKinsey, and was last an Associate Partner in the Financial Services Practice.

Gigi Ganatra has served as vice president of public relations and corporate affairs at Nordstrom since March 2017. She and her team support external and internal communications for Nordstrom and Nordstrom Rack, as well as corporate social responsibility and Nordstrom Cares.Prior to joining Nordstrom, Gigi served as vice president of communications of HSNi where she oversaw all corporate and consumer communications for the HSNi portfolio of 8 brands as well as HSNi Cares, the philanthropic arm of the company. Gigi also served as senior director of global public relations for Starwood Hotel’s design and luxury brands including St. Regis Hotels & Resorts, The Luxury Collection, W Hotels Worldwide and Le Mèridien. Prior to that, she held leadership roles at Harrison & Shriftman and Terra Group. Gigi is a leader in her field with more than twenty years of experience in strategic marketing, brand development, public relations and special events, including a rich background in fashion, design, art, travel and real estate.She served on the foundation board of Johns Hopkins All Children’s Hospital and supports numerous local charities in New York, Miami and Seattle.

Gretchen Ganc serves as Executive Vice President of Strategy and Analytics for The Container Store, where she is responsible for unlocking actionable insights, enabling data-driven decision making, and leading strategic initiatives to drive sustainable profitable growth.  Gretchen began her career in investment banking and management consulting, where she discovered her passion for consumer-focused businesses.  She has spent more than 15 years in leadership roles with multiple retailers, including JCPenney, Fossil Group, and Ann Taylor.

speaker_job_title: Global Partner Solutions Architecture Leader, Consumer Verticals
speaker_description: Krithika Ganesamoorthi is a Global AWS Solutions Architecture Leader for AWS Partners in consumer verticals. Leveraging her experience, she is responsible for solutions architecture and the technical strategy for technology and consulting partners on AWS. She recruits, nurtures, and helps partners transform their solutions on AWS. She is an advocate for partners within Amazon Retail, AWS Sales, and AWS Product teams.

Rob Garf is VP and GM for Retail at Salesforce. With more than 25 years of global retail experience as practitioner, industry analyst, strategy consultant, and software leader, he is no stranger to the industry and the challenges retailers face. He currently leads retail strategy, product, and insights -- and advises senior executives globally on digital transformation. Rob is also a frequent industry speaker, a member of NRF’s Digital Council, and Chair of the Retail Customer Advisory Board.

Jeff Gennette was elected chief executive officer of Macy's, Inc. in March 2017 and assumed the role of chairman in February 2018. Gennette previously held the role of president of Macy's, Inc. from 2014 through August 2017. Prior to that, he was the chief merchandising officer of Macy's, Inc., a position to which he was appointed in February 2009.Gennette began his retail career in 1983 as an executive trainee at Macy's West in San Francisco. He held positions of increasing responsibility, including vice president and division merchandise manager for men's collections, and senior vice president and general merchandise manager for men's and children's. In 2004, Gennette was appointed executive vice president and director of stores at Macy's Central in Atlanta. During his career, he also served as a store manager for FAO Schwarz and director of stores for Broadway Stores, Inc. From February 2006 to February 2008, Gennette served as chairman and chief executive officer of Seattle-based Macy's Northwest. Gennette returned to San Francisco from February 2008 to February 2009 as chairman and CEO of Macy's West.Gennette was appointed to the Partnership For New York City Board & Executive Committee in December 2016.In January 2018, Gennette joined the National Retail Federation Board of Directors and Executive Committee. Subsequently, in January 2019, Gennette was appointed Chairman of the NRF Foundation Board of Directors, and in June 2019 joined the NRF Finance Committee.As of July 2020, Gennette also serves on the CEO Action for Diversity & Inclusion Task Force and the Governing Committee for Racial Equity.Gennette, a native of San Diego, is a graduate of Stanford University.

In 2012 Leslie spearheaded the reimagination of fashion’s “industry bible” The TOBE Report into a consumer, culture & creative think tank decoding consumer sentiment and sociocultural influences for consumer-facing businesses in the fashion, beauty, entertainment, real estate, finance, hospitality and food & beverage industries.Joining TOBE’s parent company, The Doneger Group, in 1998 with 10 years of experience in product development, merchandising and trend analysis at specialty retailer Talbots, Leslie transformed TOBE into a spot-on projection of consumer values, commercial opportunity and marketing and messaging relevancy. She speaks regularly at conferences and corporate events, providing a fresh voice, thought- provoking topics and a thoroughly entertaining style.Out of the office, Leslie lends her industry passion to the Parsons School of Design, where she sits on the Board of Governors, and as a member of the Advisory Board for Luxury Institute’s Retail Performance Academy. She holds Bachelor of Arts degrees in English and Art History from the University of Virginia.

Ross Giambalvo is VP of Inventory Management at H-E-B, a grocery retailer proudly serving Texans since 1905 and frequently recognized by consumer research firms as the Top US Grocery Retailer & Top US eCommerce Grocery Retailer. Ross is responsible for planning and inventory management across stores, warehouses, and eCommerce. His work is focused on driving in-stocks for H-E-B Customers while improving the work experience for H-E-B Partners in stores & warehouses.
Dan Gilmore is Chief Marketing Officer at supply chain software company Softeon. He is co-author of “The Little Book of Distributed Order Management.”

Dimas Gimeno is an entrepreneur and investor in the retail and technology industry. The former executive president of El Corte Inglés, Dimas currently serves as the founding partner and CEO of WOW, a phygital retail platform that combines a marketplace and an experiential physical space.Dimas has a law degree and a master's degree in private law from the Universidad de San Pablo (CEU) and an EMBA from AESE Business School (associated with IESE).

speaker_job_title: Vice President, Supply Chain and Customs Policy
speaker_description: Jonathan Gold is vice president of supply chain and customs policy at the National Retail Federation. In this role, Gold is a primary spokesperson and is responsible for representing the retail industry before Congress and the administration on supply chain, international trade, product safety and customs related issues impacting the retail industry. While with NRF, he has been a leading advocate of the value of trade and global value chains to the U.S. economy.Prior to joining NRF, Gold served as a policy analyst in the Office of Policy and Planning for U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP). He joined CBP in May 2006 and was responsible for providing policy guidance on issues surrounding maritime cargo security and trade-related matters. Gold also worked on implementation issues surrounding the SAFE Port Act and other issues within the agency including CBP intelligence reform, pandemic flu and trade facilitation. Before joining CBP, Gold spent nearly a decade with the Retail Industry Leaders Association holding several government relations positions including director and then vice president of international trade policy before being named vice president of global supply chain policy in January 2005. Gold currently serves on the Department of Commerce’s Advisory Committee on Supply Chain Competitiveness.  He has previously served on the Department of Homeland Security’s Commercial Customs Operations Advisory Committee (COAC) and on the Department of Commerce’s International Trade Advisory Committee on Distribution Services. Gold graduated from American University in Washington, DC in 1994. He graduated with a bachelor's degree in international business with a concentration in finance.

speaker_job_title: Executive Vice President and Chief Food and Beverage Officer
speaker_description: Rick Gomez is executive vice president, chief food and beverage officer for Target Corporation. He brings deep retail expertise and more than 20 years of experience managing multi-billion-dollar consumer packaged goods brands to Target’s food and beverage business. Rick joined Target in 2013 as senior vice president of brand and category marketing, where he led marketing efforts across all merchandise categories and seasonal campaigns like holiday and back to school. He was named chief marketing officer in 2017, overseeing all of Target’s marketing and media strategy, creative, guest research, loyalty, and corporate responsibility efforts. In 2019, Rick was named head of Target’s e-commerce business and digital strategy, and later that year assumed leadership of Target’s enterprise strategy team. Prior to Target, he served as vice president of brand marketing at MillerCoors. He also held multiple leadership roles at PepsiCo, including chief marketing officer of the non-carbonated beverage portfolio. Rick began his career in brand management at the Quaker Oats Company. Rick received a B.A. from Dartmouth College. He currently serves on the Executive Council of MBOLD, a Minnesota-based organization that accelerates practical solutions to some of the biggest challenges facing food and agriculture. Rick also serves on the boards of GLSEN (Gay Lesbian Student Education Network), the National Museum of the American Latino at the Smithsonian, and The Wendy’s Corporation.

Bliss has a long career in retail. He has worked his way through just about every level of a retail organization from the sales floor to multi-unit field leader to director of operations. This has given Bliss valuable firsthand experience to better serve his clients and anticipate their needs.

Robert (“Rob”) Grimes is the Founder & CEO of the IFBTA (the Food and Beverage Technology Association), a non-profit professional association promoting and advancing technology and innovation for the global food and beverage industries.Prior to establishing the IFBTA, Rob founded FSTEC (Foodservice Technology Conference and Showcase) in 1996, ConStrata Technology Consulting, and its predecessor companies, which have been providing IT consulting services for the global hospitality, foodservice and retail industries and is headquartered in Potomac, Maryland.

speaker_job_title: Senior Vice President, Supply Chain Engineering and Product Walmart US
speaker_description: Dave joined Walmart in February of 2018 leading the Operational Excellence team in the eCommerce organization. In 2019, he was given the opportunity to direct the eCommerce Product and Engineering organizations. In 2020, he took over responsibility for the Omni-Supply Chain Product and Engineering organization. Currently, he leads the Innovation and Automation space across the US business. Over the last four years, he’s had the good fortune to build and serve small agile teams who obsess over customers, associates, and delivering business results. These efforts have resulted in better customer service, simpler processes, and have helped drive everyday low costs within the supply chain. Before joining Walmart, Dave began his career in the brewing industry in 2007 where he worked on warehouse optimization and in canning operations for Anheuser-Bush. After this experience, he joined Amazon and became a leader at their first all-apparel fulfillment center. Within Amazon’s Fulfillment Operations, he held a breadth of leadership positions including in Inbound, Outbound, Quality Control, and on Subject Matter Expert and Customer Returns teams. In 2016, he transitioned to Customer Service (CS) Operations, to develop the first Amazon Flex CS team and to lead the global Virtual Customer Service (VCS) organization, including its product, programs, and operations. In this role, he supported the team as it scaled from 1.4k to 16k employees across 11 countries. In all of his roles, his focus is on empowering teams to simplify and standardize processes and to find new ways to improve the customer and associate experience.

Matthew is a veteran retail practitioner with more than two decades of experience across software, and hardware vendors, as well as working directly for retailers. He is passionate about combining strategy, design, and technology to transform retailers and create experiences that customers remember and care about.  Today Matthew is the Global Retail Technology Strategist for Zebra. Previously he spent 10 years at Starbucks and worked for Blue Yonder Software and AT&T Wireless.

Arvind Gupta has a unique ability to critically analyze broad macroeconomic and technology trends and decompose them into tactical process improvement and IT solution development roadmaps. Arvind has held a number of leadership positions at Jesta I.S, including Chief Operating Officer, accumulating over fifteen years of retail experience. As EVP of Development and Strategy, Arvind directed the growth and expansion of Jesta’s Vision Suite ® product line and is instrumental in cementing its place as a leading ERP and retail business management system.

Barbara Hagen is the Vice President, Sales and Marketing at ThriftBooks, overseeing sales, marketing, loyalty, brand, UX, and customer service.  Prior to ThriftBooks, Barbara worked with Samsung’s South Korea executive team leading their global retail strategy. Barbara has also held marketing executive roles at Fortune 100 companies including Target, Best Buy, and Microsoft. She has a B.S. in Marketing from Syracuse University, and an MBA and M.S. in Advanced Analytics from NYU’s Stern School of Business.

Jacklyn Hammer is the Senior Director and Regional Manager with Fabletics. Jacklyn is a veteran of the retail industry with over 22 years of experience, the last 7 and a half years of which have been with Fabletics. In her tenure there, she has played a key role in building the formerly digital-only brand's presence with physical retail stores.

speaker_job_title: Vice Chair & US Leader | Retail & Consumer Products
speaker_description: Nick is Vice Chairman and leader of Deloitte LLP’s Retail and Consumer Products (RCP) practice. With over twenty-five years of experience in the industry, as well as advising Boards of Directors, executive teams, and functional leaders, Nick is a highly influential partner and thought leader.Nick’s experience includes providing strategic and operational advice in the context of turnarounds, periods of organizational crisis, and organic and inorganic value creation. He’s also often called upon for witness testimony in high profile cases in the US Federal Courts. Nick maintains a vast network of relationships across investment banks, sell-side analysts, public/investor relations, law firms, and financial advisors. When approaching client challenges and complex problems, he uses a highly pragmatic, direct, and candid consultative style rooted in uncompromising integrity and objectivity. His approach is designed to inspire confidence in clients and stakeholders.Nick also works alongside functional leaders and possesses particularly deep specialization on commercial strategy and operations including go-to-market strategy, pricing, trade promotions, shopper marketing, and diversion. Having been a retail merchant prior to his management consulting career, Nick also has a deep understanding of the inner workings of manufacturer-retailer relationships. This can be particularly helpful when he advises clients on sensitive issues requiring strong cross value-chain collaboration.Nick was twice nominated as one of the top 25 consultants by a consulting magazine and has been quoted in numerous magazines. Nick also lectures at leading business schools around the country. Nick has been very active in industry trade associations where he’s participated on key committees and led keynote presentations at marquis conferences.

Dr. Bill Hardgrave currently serves as President of the University of Memphis. In 2005, Hardgrave founded the RFID Lab which became the world’s leading center for examining the business value of RFID. He has worked with numerous companies, including Walmart, Macy’s, Dillard’s, and Target, on a variety of RFID projects. Dr. Hardgrave is an accomplished scholar—having published several books and more than 85 articles—and is considered a thought leader in the field of RFID.

Studs Co-Founder and CEO Anna Harman has a decade of extensive experience in operations, retail, finance and consumer companies known for operational excellence. In her role as Studs CEO, she sets the strategic priorities of the business and leads the company in growing all areas of the business, including operations, real estate, technology, finance, and analytics. Anna attended Princeton as an undergraduate and Boston University Law School. After practicing as an attorney and then four years at Bridgewater Associates, she transitioned to startups. As COO of Fitz, an in-home closet organizing and wardrobe styling service, Director at Store No. Eight, an incubation arm within Walmart, and the interim Chief Customer Officer at Jet Black, a text-message based personal shopping service, Anna focused on developing innovative ideas, services, operating models and technologies that make her an expert on the future of omnichannel retail.

Ron Harries is the Senior Vice President and Head of Retail for Fabletics, a TechStyle Fashion Group brand that was co-founded by Kate Hudson. Ron started his retail journey with Sears in 1987. With 30 years of retail industry experience, Ron is passionate about leading the way with Innovation & Technology that delivers a better Member and Guest experience. Ron’s focus is on driving revenue by using data to enhance relationships.

speaker_job_title: Vice President, Enterprise Solutions Vertical Markets
Chief Marketing, Digital and Omni Officer
speaker_description: Jacob Hawkins is the Chief Marketing, Digital and Omni Officer at Forever 21. He is an innovative executive and inspirational leader with a strong track record of driving business growth and profitability while delivering a world-class customer experience. His areas of expertise include people development, team building, omnichannel, e-commerce, marketing, merchandising, operations, logistics, supply chain, loyalty, analytics, business development and customer experience.Prior to joining Forever 21, Hawkins was the Executive Vice President, Chief Marketing Officer for Belk, where he helped transform the company's digital omni business. During his tenure, some of the key achievements he helped Belk accomplish included shifting the vast majority of advertising spend from offline to digital; relaunching and elevating the Belk brand; improving NPS customer satisfaction; launching BOPIS and growing it to more than 20% of sales; launching ship-from-store and growing it to more 60% of sales; launching curbside pickup and same-day delivery; leveraging AI to determine where best to ship online orders from; and dramatically growing the dropship business. Prior to joining Belk, Hawkins helped brands such as Groupon, Aéropostale and Overstock to transform and grow their businesses.

David Helms is the Chief Technology Officer at Radius Networks, where he leads new product initiatives and guides the company’s product and technical strategy. He has over 25 years of experience in product management, professional services, software development and hardware engineering at industry leading companies including Check Point Software, Bay Networks and IBM. Mr. Helms is a recognized expert on mobile proximity technology and is a frequent speaker at major industry conferences and events.Prior to his role at Radius Networks, Mr. Helms was the Vice President of CyberSecurity at Salient Federal Solutions, where he led the company’s effort to develop cyber security products and services for US Government organizations.

speaker_job_title: Executive Vice President and Chief Growth Officer
speaker_description: Christina Hennington is executive vice president and chief growth officer for Target and a member of its leadership team. A forward-thinking enterprise leader, Christina leads teams responsible for Target’s strategy, several enterprise capabilities, including the Target in India team, and all of merchandising, which includes the retailer’s best-in-class product design and sourcing organizations. As chief growth officer, she works across the organization to identify and pursue revenue-generating strategies that deepen the company’s relevancy with current and prospective guests, and reinforce its stronghold as a leading U.S. retailer. Prior to this role, Christina held several merchandising leadership roles and supply chain positions at Target since joining in 2003. Additionally, Christina has been a strong champion of D&I efforts as a sponsor for several employee resource groups over the years and a founding member of the Racial Equity Action and Change committee (REACH) in 2020. In this role, she helps lead the organization’s strategy to drive lasting impact for our Black team members, guests and communities.Christina has been consistently recognized by the industry for her leadership and continued focus on diversity, technology and guest experience. She has been named Merchandiser of the Year and one of the 50 Most Influential People in the Multicultural Market by Women’s Wear Daily, one of the 50 Outstanding Women in Retail by RetailToday, and one of the Most Influential Women in Retail by Mass Market Retailers. She currently serves on the Board of THOR Industries. She also serves on the Board for Second Harvest Heartland, one of the nation’s largest hunger relief organizations, and served as the board chair in 2020. She is a Henry Crown Fellow of the Aspen Institute. She previously served on the board of governors for Cosmetic Executive Women (CEW).

speaker_job_title: Senior Vice President & Head of Sustainability for J. Crew Group, Senior Vice President of Sourcing for Madewell
speaker_description: Liz Hershfield is Senior Vice President & Head of Sustainability for J. Crew Group, as well as Senior Vice President of Sourcing for Madewell. Ms. Hershfield joined the company in October of 2019, focusing on Madewell, overseeing Sourcing & Sustainability. In February of 2021, Ms. Hershfield expanded her scope to oversee all Sustainability & CSR efforts for J. Crew Group (J.Crew, Madewell & J Crew Factory). Ms. Hershfield has 26 years of deep industry experience across Sourcing, Product Development, Supply Chain/Operations & Sustainability. Prior to joining Madewell, Ms. Hershfield held the position of Chief Supply Chain Officer and Vice President of Sourcing & Production at Bonobos, where she was a founding leader, building infrastructure across sourcing, quality, product development, & operations, in order to support accelerated growth, ultimately leading to the sale of the brand in 2017 to Walmart. From there, Ms. Hershfield moved into the role Head of Product Development, Production and Sustainability on the Walmart eCommerce team before joining the Madewell business.

Steve Hewitt, Executive Chair, joined Gymshark in June 2014 as an external advisor, before becoming Managing Director the following year, having previously served as CEO at Sport Brands Group and EMEA commercial director at Reebok. Prior to Ben Francis taking over as CEO, Steve became CEO of Gymshark in 2017, spearheading the development of the senior leadership team, which has been responsible for the exponential growth of the brand on a global scale.

Jeff Hill is the Sr. Director of Supply Chain at Titan Brands, a Direct-to-Consumer eCommerce Retailer.  Jeff was the 4th employee of Titan and has overseen all areas of the business from Accounting, Warehouse Operations, Planning and Procurement, as well as Information Technology and Systems.  With over 15 years of eCommerce experience, he has been responsible for implementing most of Titan’s enterprise software.

Gerri Hinkel is Director of Solutions and Vertical marketing. Over her 30-year career, she has developed a passion for how technology helps people and organizations.  Her focus at Aruba is to help various industries re-define the role of the network from a foundational "connector" of things to an enabler for driving improved operations and business outcomes. In addition to industry marketing, she enjoys bringing technology to life through the production of Aruba’s technical keynote addresses. Gerri holds a BS in mathematics from Colorado State University and an M.Ed. from the University of Texas at Brownsville.

Jeffrey Hogue is chief sustainability officer for Levi Strauss & Co., the iconic jeanswear brand with a 167-year history. In his role, he leads the sustainability and circular economy efforts for the organization to create positive impact across the environmental and social aspects of their global operations. Before joining LS&Co., Mr. Hogue held several CSO and lead roles in a variety of industries, including biotechnology, food and agriculture, retail food, and fashion. He designed and led C&A’s global sustainability strategies, leading C&A to become the world’s largest user of certified organic cotton, guiding the organization to create circular knit products certified to Cradle to CradleTM GOLD, and guiding the organization to leadership positions in transparency and prevention of forced labor and slavery. Before his role at C&A, Mr. Hogue led the CSR and sustainability team at McDonald’s Corp, where he led the development of McDonald’s first global sustainability strategy and climate change positions. Earlier he served for 17 years at Danisco A/S & Genencor International and led the integration of Danisco’s sustainability and regulatory programs into the DuPont organization. He has held numerous nonprofit board positions, including with the C&A Foundation, the Innovation Committee of Fashion for Good, Textile Exchange, Organic Cotton Accelerator, Sun Valley Institute, Presidio Graduate School and The Cradle to Cradle Product Innovation Institute.

Jason Holland serves as President of Firework. He has been integral to the company’s rapid rise in profile across the globe. Jason draws from a 20-plus-year wellspring of experience as a leader, innovator, and driver of change within the digital media ecosystem, including the company’s recent industry-first partnership with grocery giant Albertsons, Inc. Prior to joining Firework, Jason served as Global COO and Partner at Evolve Media LLC, where he remains an Advisor to the business.

speaker_job_title: Head of Worldwide Consumer Products & Retail GTM
speaker_description: Justin Honaman leads the worldwide retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) go-to-market team at Amazon Web Services (AWS). He is also the worldwide segment leader for food & beverage. His team’s focus within retail and CPG is on delivering supply chain, ecommerce, data and analytics, and digital engagement business solutions for customers globally.

speaker_job_title: Global Vice President, Retail Marketing & Merchandising
speaker_description: Kristin is Global Vice President within SAP’s Retail Industry Business Unit and is responsible for the retail strategy and roadmap across the areas of merchandising and predictive analytics. She is a trusted advisor for SAP’s global retail customer base. In her prior roles with Accenture, Deloitte Consulting, and Khimetrics, Kristin has leveraged her skills as a management consultant to lead technology transformation projects at some of the world’s most recognized retail brands.

Neal Hubman is an early founder of Reddit's Brand Partnerships team. With a vision for the opportunity ahead for Reddit and its partners, Neal helped to architect the playbook widely used today by brands seeking to authentically engage across the platform. Over the past six years, he has launched many of Reddit’s most integral teams, all the while helping emerging and digital native brands harness the untapped potential of Reddit’s 52 million daily active users and 100,000+ online communities.  In his current role, Neal leads Global Client Solutions which comprises Creative Strategy (KarmaLab), Measurement, Product Specialists, and Partner Enablement teams.  Before joining Reddit, Neal was part of the first team at Google focused on assisting mobile-first companies launch user acquisition campaigns with Adwords. A native of Fresno, California, Neal now lives in the Bay Area with his wife and three children.

Caroline Hyde anchors Bloomberg Television’s “The Close" from New York. She has also served as the network’s chief European correspondent, traveling the region to report on the most important business, technology, economic and political issues shaping global markets.

Robert Irvine is a world-class chef, entrepreneur, and philanthropist. The host of Food Network’s hit show Restaurant: Impossible, he has given struggling restaurateurs a second chance to turn their lives and businesses around in over 200 episodes and counting. He would know a thing or two about running a successful business. In addition to his restaurants—Robert Irvine’s Public House at the Tropicana in Las Vegas and Fresh Kitchen by Robert Irvine within the Pentagon—he is the owner of FitCrunch, whose protein bars, powders, and snacks are available nationwide; Robert Irvine Foods, which makes prepared, restaurant-quality dishes available in grocery stores and provides food service to military bases worldwide; and the Lansdale, PA-based Boardroom Spirits, creators of handcrafted vodka, rum, whiskey, and more.A portion of the proceeds from all of Robert’s endeavors benefit the Robert Irvine Foundation. Created in 2014, the foundation gives back to our servicemen and women and first responders. Funds raised help at-need veterans and first responders in a variety of ways: training service dogs, making mental health and wellness services available, providing mobility devices for the disabled, and much more. Learn more at RobertIrvineFoundation.org.

speaker_job_title: Retail and Consumer Goods Product Marketing Lead
speaker_description: Sara Isaacson is a Product Marking Lead at Salesforce focused exclusively on MuleSoft within the retail and consumer goods industries. Sara spent 5 years in the beauty industry working on developing and launching product innovation.

Grant Isley is Sr. Director at Körber Supply Chain Software, focusing on SaaS Operations with clients using Enspire Commerce Order Management, Point of Sale, Vendor Dropship, Product Information Management, and/or Trading Partner Management. Grant has 8 years’ experience across Enspire Implementation, Solution Architecting, Support, and Client Success with leading retailers, manufacturers, and 3PLs. His passion is identifying, solving, and preventing problems impacting Körber’s clients and their end customers.

Stephen Jackson is the Executive Vice President & Chief Information Officer for Harry Rosen Inc.  In his capacity as CIO, he has overall responsibilities for Information Technology strategy and sets priorities and direction in the areas of Service, Project Management, Infrastructure, Security and Development.  Prior to Harry Rosen’s, he was Director of I/S at a leading international publishing company.  He has more than 30 years of IT experience including several Customer Relationship Management system implementations.

Jon serves as the Vice President of the Just Walk Out Stores. His team is responsible for building the technologies and services that enable Amazon’s groundbreaking autonomous retail solution – Just Walk Out technology by Amazon. During his first stint at Amazon Jon worked on the retail website content optimization team, led the builder tools organization, and founded the team that created the Silk web browser for the Amazon Fire tablet. Jon took a break from Amazon to lead the engineering team at Pinterest and to found his own startup (Hestan Smart Cooking) before returning to Amazon in 2022. He earned BA in political science from Depauw University.

People and business leader with over 30 years of experience at world class brands including adidas, The Walt Disney Company and Mattel, Inc. Passionate about delivering strategic brand and product vision through leadership, innovation and an uncompromising focus on the consumer and the highest quality service to the customer.

Roshan is the General Manager of Retail at Square. His vision is to empower retailers of all sizes to thrive, by leveraging world class design and Square’s unique platform approach. He oversees all aspects of this business vertical including the experience of retailers who run their businesses on Square's software and hardware. Prior to 2016, Roshan held a variety of leadership roles in quantitative finance and investment banking in the NYC area. In his free time he enjoys motorcycling, playing guitar and karaoke with his two children.

speaker_job_title: VP, Asset Protection and Retail Operations

speaker_description: Jen Jones is the Chief Marketing Officer at commercetools, the leading digital commerce provider behind some of the largest and most innovative global brands such as Lululemon, Sephora, L.L. Bean, Audi, and Ulta. She oversees commercetools’ global brand, marketing, and corporate communications efforts. Prior to joining commercetools in 2022, Jones was the Chief Marketing Officer at Dataminr, the leading real-time information discovery platform, where she was responsible for all of the company's brand, marketing, and corporate communications initiatives. She also previously served as Senior Vice President of Global Marketing at Cision, and Director of Industry Product Marketing at Oracle Marketing Cloud group.

speaker_job_title: SVP, Boutiques, Real Estate, and Strategic Partnerships
speaker_description: Chris Kaighn is the SVP of Boutiques, Real Estate & Strategic Partnerships at Francesca's, a specialty women’s retailer offering unique, free-spirited fashion, jewelry, and lifestyle products.Prior to joining Francesca's, Chris served as the SVP of Stores & Store Operations at Justice & Limited Too.Chris is passionate about building and empowering highly effective, engaged and diverse teams. She’s committed to associate and customer empathy as the foundation for strategy and achieving results.

Hana Kajimura leads sustainability at Allbirds, pushing the brand to integrate environmental and social sustainability into all aspects of the business including product, supply chain, and operations. She also shapes Allbirds’s innovation narrative and storytelling, translating sustainability initiatives into engaging consumer-facing stories.

Dr. Kalish is the Chief Global Economist of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Ltd. He is a specialist in global economic issues as well as the effects of economic, demographic, and social trends on the global business environment. He advises Deloitte clients as well as Deloitte’s leadership on economic issues and their impact on business strategy. In addition, he has given numerous presentations to corporations and trade organizations on topics related to the global economy. He is widely traveled and has given presentations in 47 countries on six continents. He has been quoted by the Wall Street Journal, The Economist, and The Financial Times. Dr. Kalish holds a bachelor’s degree in economics from Vassar College and a PhD in international economics from Johns Hopkins University.

speaker_job_title: Executive Vice President, Chief Human Resources Officer
speaker_description: Jordana has served as Executive Vice President, Chief Human Resources Officer of Claire's since February 2020. Prior to this, she was at Core-Mark, serving as Chief Human Resources Officer, Senior Vice President from 2017 to 2020. Prior to Core-Mark, she was at SC Johnson, serving as Vice President, Human Resources, Global Commercial Division from 2014 to 2017, Vice President, Human Resources, North America from 2012 to 2014 and Director, Human Resources, North America Product Supply from 2010 to 2012. She previously held positions at American Express and DaimlerChrysler. Jordana received a BS in Sociology from the University of Wisconsin-La Crosse and an MS in Industrial Relations from the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

RELEX Group CEO Mikko Kärkkäinen was the driving force behind the company’s inception in 2005. He met co-founders Johanna Småros and Michael Falk at Helsinki University of Technology, where he earned a Ph.D. in Supply Chain Management. Mikko oversees operations, helping customers improve their forecasting and replenishment processes. Additionally, he has authored over thirty papers on supply chain issues and was most recently named one of The Top 50 SaaS CEOs by The Software Report.

Vijay Karthik serves as SVP and Chief Technology Officer at Neiman Marcus Group, where he oversees engineering, architecture, and technology operations. He has been with Neiman Marcus Group for over 5 years, championing cloud adoption, application modernization, product operating model transformation, and architecture evolution. Vijay has a wide range of professional experience in CPG, healthcare, telecommunication, inflight broadband, B2B, and resale/remarketing. Vijay has an undergraduate and post-graduate degree from Birla Institute of Technology and Science – Pilani, India.

Frank leads PayPal’s Enterprise & Merchant Platform segment, which helps large and mid-sized merchants grow their business. His teams are responsible for the P&L, strategy, and execution of Checkout, Payment Processing with Braintree and PPCP, Payouts / Hyperwallet, Post Purchase (Happy Returns, Package Tracking) and Growth Markets (Japan, Mexico, Brazil, and product delivery for China). Frank has been with PayPal since 2011. He has a doctorate degree in Software Engineering from the Hasso Plattner Institute.

Mary Kellmanson was named Chief Digital Marketing Officer of 99 Cents Only Stores in February of 2021 and is responsible for all aspects of omnichannel marketing and brand strategy. Kellmanson brings to The 99 over 25 years as a senior Marketing Executive with expertise in developing branding strategies for public and private companies. She began her career with Wegmans Food Markets and has subsequently served as the top marketing executive for Southeastern Grocers, The Fresh Market, and Web.com. Kellmanson holds a MBA from the University of Rochester and BA in Government from Franklin & Marshall College.

Andy Kettlewell serves as Vice President of Inventory Management, Master Data Management and Supply Chain Planning for Walgreens, supporting enterprise master data management, forecasting, procurement, planning, and inventory flow for 200MM item store combinations supporting >$35B in annual sales. During his tenure at Walgreens, Andy has led over $1B in working capital improvement while improving store in stock condition by 80%. He's also reduced operational costs thru advanced analytics, strategic partnerships, and talent development.

Joan King is Executive Vice President, Digital and International for Crate & Barrel Holdings.  She leads Digital strategy, digital experience design, ecommerce operations with full responsibility for the channel.  Her team is responsible for delivering a seamless and inspirational digital experience across Crate and Barrel’s brands on all devices, apps and in-store solutions.  Additionally, she oversees International Strategy and Operations for the Crate & Barrel brands. Joan started her career at Crate & Barrel in 2001. As the business grew and evolved, so did her responsibilities as she built key functions along the way from web analytics and AB testing, SEO, digital UX and product management, CGI and digital merchandising. Prior to Crate and Barrel, she built and managed the UX team of Organic Online's Chicago office, where she led the user experience design of many first-generation commerce web sites.  Her passion is to create experiences customers love.  She has a BA in English from Boston College, an MA in Writing from DePaul University and has completed all coursework towards her Ph.D. at the University of Illinois in Chicago.

speaker_job_title: Global Director, Industry Strategy & Marketing - Retail, Travel and Consumer Goods
speaker_description: Michael Klein is the Head of Industry Strategy - Retail, Travel & CPG - for Adobe Systems. He is responsible for Adobe’s point-of-view and messaging in the commerce verticals, and works with Adobe’s global commerce clients to help them develop best-in-class digital marketing strategies. Michael is a retail industry leader, regularly contributing to industry events. His vast experience includes over 25 years as a Senior Merchant and Marketer for multiple brands including William-Sonoma, Harry & David, Discovery Channel Stores, eLuxury.com (LVMH Group), and wine.com.

As Vice President and Principal Analyst at Forrester, Sucharita is a leading expert on eCommerce, multichannel retail, consumer behavior, and trends in the online shopping space. In her research, Sucharita covers such consumer-oriented topics as eCommerce forecasting and trends, merchandising best practices, conversion optimization, and social computing in the retail world.Prior to Forrester, Sucharita was the director of marketing at Saks Fifth Avenue, where she managed the customer acquisition, retention, and market research efforts for the $2 billion luxury retailer's online channel. Sucharita holds a B.A. in economics from Harvard University and an M.B.A. from the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

speaker_job_title: SVP, Global Ops & Digital Supply Chain Data, Analytics & Automation
speaker_description: Sasha Pailet Koff, is the Senior Vice President, Digital Supply Chain with Dell Technologies. Sasha leads the Data, Analytics and Automation (DAA) function for Dell’s Global Operation organization. DAA enables Dell’s Supply Chain through predictive analytics, streamlining business processes through automation, and enabling full data transparency.Prior to joining Dell in 2022, Sasha spent 20 years with Johnson & Johnson, most recently serving as Vice President of Supply Chain Technology for Consumer Health.

speaker_job_title: Senior Partner and Global Leader of Consumer Goods and Retail Practice
speaker_description: Sajal Kohli is a Senior Partner at McKinsey & Company, based in Chicago, and leads McKinsey’s global work in the retail and consumer-goods industries.In more than 25 years of management consulting, Sajal has counseled leading global retail and packaged-goods clients as they set growth strategies and face the challenges of both the changing international landscape and domestic-market shifts because of digitization, consolidation, and new entrants. He has served retail and consumer-goods clients across Asia, Canada, Europe, Latin America, and North America. He serves retail clients across multiple formats, including hypermarkets, pure ecommerce players, grocers, restaurant chains, department stores, and leading consumer-goods companies across food, consumer-electronics, personal-care, beauty, and other categories.Sajal focuses on working with senior leaders and their management teams to drive growth and commercial and operations transformations across both retail and consumer-goods companies. Over the last few years he has been spending a lot of time with organizations on the potential and implications of tech enablement, including advanced analytics, artificial intelligence, and their implications on the future of work.Before joining McKinsey, Sajal was an executive for a retail and consumer-goods company in Asia and Europe. On a personal level, Sajal has accomplished milestones such as climbing Mount Kilimanjaro, completing several Chicago Olympic distance triathlons, playing in the US Squash Illinois State Championships, and was a national swimmer and part of the Indian junior water-polo team. As a global citizen who has lived and worked across continents, Sajal is also deeply invested in the power of foreign policy and is a trustee of the Chicago Council on Global Affairs.

Shameek Konar serves as Pilot Company’s chief executive officer. Headquartered in Knoxville, Tennessee, Pilot Company is the 7th largest privately held company in the United States and employs 28,000 team members. Founded in 1958, Pilot Company has grown to become one of the leading suppliers of fuel and is the largest operator of travel centers in North America. The company supplies more than 14 billion gallons of fuel per year and has an industry-leading network of more than 800 fueling locations. With the third largest tanker fleet in North America, Pilot Company also provides end-to-end logistics solutions, gasoline, diesel and crude hauling, and saltwater disposal in the nation’s busiest oil production basins.  Shameek previously served as Pilot Company’s chief strategy officer, leading the company’s energy division, including business and asset development, supply, distribution and sales. His leadership was instrumental in growing Pilot Company’s energy business, developing the platform and executing several acquisitions since his tenure at Pilot Company began in 2017. Prior to joining Pilot Company, Shameek served as chief investment officer at Castleton Commodities International and Mercuria Energy Trading in Houston. He oversaw mergers, acquisitions and operations of physical assets across the firm’s commodities merchant platform globally. He also was managing director and global co-head of commodities principal investments at Goldman Sachs, where he focused on growing the company’s physical natural gas and crude oil businesses. Shameek served in several senior roles at Constellation Energy, including senior vice president of corporate strategy and development.Shameek earned a Ph.D. in economics and finance from Vanderbilt University in Nashville and a Bachelor of Arts in economics and mathematics from St. Stephen’s College in India.

speaker_job_title: IT Director, Ecommerce and Digital Systems
speaker_description: Omar Koncobo is IT Director, eCommerce and Digital Systems at Ulta Beauty. He has nearly 20 years of experience in the IT field and has operated in various IT disciplines in multiple industries including trading, insurance, pharmacy and retail.Omar is also a coffee farmer, but loves tea. His favorite sport is soccer or football depending on which part of the world you come from.

Kelly Kovack is the quietly loud renegade of the beauty industry, with 25 years of experience and an ability for intuiting emerging trends. Leaning right but fueled by left-brain thinking, her career is the culmination of identifying white space, developing category-defining strategies, and building brands from conceptual start-ups to tactical cleanups. Kelly is someone who makes things happen.  Kelly started her career in beauty as a member of the original executive team of Bliss. She also joined MD Skincare (Dr. Dennis Gross Skincare) in its nascent stage, and was responsible for the foundational branding and marketing strategies that positioned the brand as one of the premier cosmeceutical brands in both the retail and professional arenas. As an advisor and strategist, Kelly has consulted dozens of organizations of all sizes, from emerging start-ups to iconic institutions including multibillion-dollar brands like Old Navy, Banana Republic, Procter & Gamble, Estée Lauder, and Mattel, as well as notable independents like Carol’s Daughter. Kelly has also created and built her own brands. The most recent among these ventures is the award-winning fragrance brand Odin co-founded in collaboration with the New York fashion brand.Kelly launched BeautyMatter in the fall of 2016 with the firm belief that in a fast-moving world, information is knowledge, but context matters. And more importantly, innovation comes from the cross-pollination of industries and expertise. She set out to use her experience building brands and scaling businesses to offer a new perspective and holistic approach to B2B content, filling a void in the beauty industry. Kovack serves on the Board of the Independent Beauty Association and is a go-to resource on the beauty industry, emerging trends, and branding. Kelly frequently shares expertise and insights with major media publications and institutions, and as a speaker at conferences globally.

As Chief Platform Officer, Kelly’s role is focused on leading her team to build revolutionary multi-channel e-commerce solutions and operating e-commerce sites for luxury brands and retailers. Kelly sits on the FARFETCH Executive Board.Kelly joined FARFETCH in 2010 to build and lead all digital marketing initiatives including customer acquisition, retention and engagement. Within her time at FARFETCH, she also led the international growth and development strategy, overseeing expansion and localization for all markets. Kelly’s background is in internet marketing and e-commerce, with her experience spanning over 15 years.

Steve Kramer is the CEO and Co-founder of WorkJam and serves on its board of directors.He has +20 years of executive leadership experience driving results and developing disruptive technologies for the retail industry, and manages WorkJam’s overall operations.Beyond WorkJam, Steve is a board member with CSNews’ Diversity & Inclusion Advisory, Cummings Centre, Nacelle, and Naveo Commerce.Steve holds a Bachelor of Commerce degree from McGill University and lives with his family in Montreal.

Julian Krenge is co-founder and CTO of parcelLab. He is passionate about creating tech that is functional, seamless and well-built to fulfill needs. As CTO, Julian's focus remains on the product: its development, vision and how it can connect people and brands. In just five years, Julian has grown the platform from parcelLab's first customer to now over 550+ customers operating in 153 countries, sending over 13 million emails a day.

Millie Kresevich is Senior Director of Asset Protection for EssilorLuxottica. Millie has worked in Big Box, Corporate, and Specialty. Millie holds an AAS in Criminal Justice, BS in Social Psychology (Suma Cum Laude), and a Masters in Business Ethics and Compliance. Millie has several published articles on the topic of workplace dishonesty and has been interviewed for many magazines and papers. In addition, she co-authored a book titled: Retail Security and Loss Prevention Solutions.  She serves on the LP Council for the National Retail Federation (NRF) and is a member of the Loss Prevention Research Council (LPRC) with the University of Florida. She has contributed to the development of certification programs for the Loss Prevention Foundation and has been a speaker on loss prevention topics nationally and internationally. In 2020, Millie was the recipient of the Magpie award given by Loss Prevention Magazine for Excellence in Leadership.  Previously, Millie also served as a mentor for under-privileged women through the Dress for Success program.

Suresh Krishna joined Northern Tool + Equipment as CEO in April 2020 after five years as a member of the Advisory Board. He brings entrepreneurial leadership and innovation skills to help propel the next phase of growth for Northern. Since taking over as CEO, Suresh has led the organization to revenue growth of 40%, serving a record number of customers, and generated record EBITA in a short two-year period. Suresh’s approach to business is forward looking, results oriented and decisive, with an emphasis on speed in execution to maximize Northern’s relevance to customers.Prior to joining Northern, Krishna held senior leadership roles at leading global companies including Polaris, United Technologies, Diageo and Sleep Number. He has vast experience in driving innovation, implementing digital transformation, building flexible supply chains and leading mergers and acquisitions. Krishna’s broad and strategic mindset and ability to drive transformative change position him to help Northern achieve its potential.Suresh holds an M.B.A. from the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University in Evanston, IL, and a B.S. in mechanical engineering from the National Institute of Technology in India. Suresh is a regular speaker at the Carlson School of Management at the University of Minnesota and the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University. He is also a member of the Minnesota Business Partnership, and Wall Street Journal CEO Council.
Satish Kumar is VP of Client Services at supply chain software company Softeon, and recognized Order Management System expert. He is co-author of “The Little Book of Distributed Order Management.”

Atif is the founder and CEO of Vistry. Vistry is focused on AI for Restaurant Automation - which provide restaurateurs insights and automation services needed to address their digital and labor shortage challenges. Vistry’s platform leverages vision, voice and IoT data analytics to help measure and improve speed, production quality and guest experience.

Sarah LaFleur is the Founder & CEO of M.M.LaFleur. Sarah founded M.M.LaFleur in 2013 with a mission to help women take the work out of getting dressed, so they can focus on the work that matters to them. Prior to founding M.M.LaFleur, she worked at Bain & Co. in New York and TechnoServe in South Africa. Sarah sits on the board of International Rescue Committee's Airbel Impact Lab and on the business advisory council of the New York Fed. She graduated from Harvard University. M.M.LaFleur is named after her mother, whom she calls her biggest inspiration.

Sivakumar (Siva) Lakshmanan is the Vice President and General Manager of antuit.ai, now part of Zebra Technologies. He is responsible for driving product strategy, client success and thought leadership. Recognized as an industry expert on driving supply chain transformation via advanced analytics, Siva has advised multiple Fortune 500 companies on demand forecasting, enterprise supply chain planning, network design, and inventory optimization across CPG, retail, and manufacturing.

speaker_job_title: Sr. Director, Associate Experience, Communication, Store Technology & Innovation
speaker_description: Marc Lambo is the Vice President of Store Operations at American Eagle Outfitters & Aerie, a leading global specialty retailer offering high-quality, on-trend clothing, accessories and personal care products at affordable prices. Marc joined the company in March 2007 as store manager and held several store operations position progressing over the 15+ years at AEO and the consumer retail sector.

speaker_job_title: VP, Customer Care, Logistics & Field Operations
speaker_description: Philippe Lambotte is a global supply chain leader with experience of working in complex environments across industries. Phillipe has led extensive leadership assignments across operations, supply chain, logistics, product development/launch, engineering, program management and customer services.With a proven track record he has demonstrated results in implementing large digital transformations and change management initiatives. Phillipe is passionate about customer and supplier collaboration.

Molly Langenstein is CEO and President of Chico’s FAS. Before joining the company, she served as General Business Manager for Ready-to-Wear at Macy’s, Inc., Chief Private Brands Officer of Macy’s and Bloomingdale's, and EVP of Private Brands for Men’s and Children’s Wear at Macy’s Private Brands. Molly is a 30-year retail industry veteran with a proven track record of building multiple successful brands and a record of success revitalizing sales and profitability in the retail fashion industry.

Maria Latushkin is an innovative technology executive who has spent more than 20 years leading Product, Engineering and Operations teams, transforming technology for global brands including Walmart.com, Peet’s Coffee, and most recently, Albertsons Cos.  As Group Vice President, Technology & Engineering, Maria is responsible for all aspects of technology for Retail and Supply Chain, thinking through the future of retail technology and spearheading the digital transformation of the retail and supply chain space. Her work focuses on strengthening Albertsons Cos. core technology foundation to drive transformation and productivity across all major functions.  Prior to her current position Maria held CTO roles at various consumer technology companies in the consumer and healthcare space, including Omada Health and One Kings Lane.

speaker_job_title: Executive Vice President & Chief Operating Officer
speaker_description: Andrew Laudato is the author of Fostering Innovation: How to Build an Amazing IT Team. He started his career as a computer programmer and quickly realized he was as passionate about business as technology. Laudato currently serves as the Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer of The Vitamin Shoppe. In this role, he leads operations across digital commerce, information technology, enterprise portfolio management, supply chain, strategic sourcing, quality, and commercialization.

Sara leads Zebra’s Global Software Customer Success team, a team of subject matter experts from various industries and functional areas.  Sara has over 25 years in Retail, Wholesale Sales and Operations. Prior, she was Director of Multi-Channel Operations at Vera Bradley, where she led a workforce of Sales teams and Key Account Managers.  Sara brings a deep understanding of what it takes to prioritize, implement, and sustain success in a variety of retail business models.

speaker_job_title: EVP and Global GM, SAP Consumer Industries
speaker_description: Matt Laukaitis is the EVP and Global General Manager of SAP’s Consumer Industries organization, and is responsible for the group’s people and business strategies, solutions portfolio, partner ecosystem, performance, and customer success globally. Since joining SAP as an account executive in 2004, Matt has held a variety of positions of increasing responsibility, including SVP and MD of SAP’s Consumer Industries in North America. Along the way, he has been fortunate to work with a tremendous team of dedicated professionals helping SAP’s customers to be successful in attaining measurable outcomes through digital transformations.Prior to SAP, Matt worked in sales and sales leadership positions at IONA Technologies, Netfish (IONA Technologies), RR Donnelley, and Apple. Matt holds a Bachelor of Science degree in mechanical engineering from Lehigh University in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, and an MBA from the Thunderbird School of Global Management, in Phoenix, Arizona.Matt serves on the board of the City of Bellevue Chamber of Commerce (WA), the advisory board of the St. Joseph’s University Academy of Food Marketing (PA), the board of governors of the Fashion Scholarship Fund (NY), and the industry advisory board of the Runway of Dreams Foundation (NJ), as well as the Lehigh University Parent Committee.

As the PIM Product Owner at URBN Inc, Morgan Lawrence is responsible for the growth and success of STEP. With eight years of experience in the PIM space across multiple industries, she is passionate about creating the best data experience.

Michael is a senior retail advisor and consumer growth consultant, and he has been on the front lines of retail industry change for his entire career. He has delivered keynotes, hosted fire-side discussions and participated worldwide in thought leadership panels. Michael brings 25+ years of brand/retail/marketing & eCommerce leadership experience with Levi's, Black & Decker, Hudson's Bay, and Pandora Jewelry.    Michael also produces a network of leading retail trade podcasts, including the Remarkable Retail podcast.

David’s focus over the last 20 years has been helping companies achieve growth, profitability, and exit objectives, primarily in media, entertainment, and technology.  With deep experience in building product, technology, and operational capabilities for both start-up and established companies, David has held both advisory and operational roles for private equity and their portfolio companies.  Currently, David is the Chief Information Officer for Careismatic Brands, a leading manufacturer of medical scrubs.

Nicole Leinbach is the Founder of RetailMinded.com, a retail industry resource that has been recognized worldwide for its leading business insight since 2007, and the bi-annual Independent Retailer Conference held in Las Vegas. With a core concentration on supporting retailers, small business operators, online sellers and the various touchpoints of commerce that influence modern merchants, Leinbach is a frequent guest and contributor to various media outlets that have included The Today Show, Forbes, Entreprenuer.com and countless B2B publications. Additionally, Leinbach has supported American Express’s Small Business Saturday as a Spokesperson, is the Author of the book “Retail 101: The Guide to Managing and Marketing Your Retail Business” from McGraw-Hill and is an instructor at Columbia College Chicago in their retail business program. She also recently co-wrote the children’s book “Shadow Siblings” with her daughter and has been recognized within the top 10 out 100 worldwide retail thought leaders since 2015 – including having held the #1 spot. Additionally, in March 2022, WWD recognized Leinbach as one of 25 Inspirational Women Leaders in Business. Leinbach is Denver based, mom to 4 children and 3 dogs and an avid traveler.

Andrew Lipsman is a Principal Analyst at Insider Intelligence, focusing on retail and ecommerce. Recent coverage includes grocery ecommerce, retail media networks, D2C brands, social commerce, mobile retail apps, holiday shopping, and Amazon Prime Day.Previously, Andrew was SVP of Marketing & Insights at comScore, covering multiple industries and overseeing the company’s global marketing insights and thought leadership initiatives. He has specialized in several coverage areas in addition to retail, including social media, mobile, digital advertising and cross-platform marketing. Andrew has been a regular contributor to the Journal of Advertising Research and is frequently quoted by leading news organizations like the New York Times, Wall Street Journal, Advertising Age, CNN, Fortune, Reuters and Bloomberg.

speaker_job_title: Vice President and Head of Supply Chain
speaker_description: Carlos Londono is currently Vice President and Head of Supply Chain for Chipotle Mexican Grill. In this role Carlos is responsible for all aspects of supply chain including direct and indirect procurement, logistics, planning and continuous improvement. Before Chipotle, Carlos was the global Vice President of Supply Chain for Owens-Illinois Inc. (O-I) headquartered in Perrysburg, Ohio.  O-I is a $6 Billion global manufacturing company that specializes in glass container products for industries such as food, beverages, and pharmaceuticals.  It is the world's largest manufacturer of glass containers. O-I, its affiliates, or licensees make approximately one out of every two glass containers in the world. In his role at O-I, Carlos was responsible for optimizing Procurement, Logistics and Supply Chain planning and execution for Europe, North America, Latin America and Asia Pacific. This includes managing a complex array of products and customers in a global value chain.Before O-I, Carlos led the Coffee, Tea, Food and Merchandising supply chain area for Starbucks Coffee Company in Seattle.  While at Starbucks, Carlos led numerous improvement initiatives in the integrated value network and developed world-class supply chain capabilities for these business units. Carlos was instrumental in the integration of acquisitions like Tazo, Teavana, La Boulange and Evolution Fresh.  He developed the Starbucks consumer packaged goods supply chain in the U.S. and Canada and implemented the Integrated Business Planning Process.Prior to his Starbucks role, Carlos led the integrated supply chain planning functions at Kraft Heinz Company and was responsible for developing their core supply chain capabilities and global strategy. Carlos received his industrial engineering degree from Universidad Eafit in Medellín, Colombia, and his MBA from Wake Forest University in North Carolina. He is fluent in Spanish, English, German, and Mandarin.

Jacqueline Long is the Director of Merchandise Process at Bealls, Inc, where she has won several awards for her work in process improvement and collaboration. She has 13 years of corporate retail experience with a focus around planning and allocation, replenishment, system administration, and corporate strategy implementation through system and process design. Long earned an MBA from Marshall University and is currently serving as the Chair for SAS Retail Users Group.

Chris Ludy is the Senior Vice President, Digital, Food Services for Compass Digital, the innovation and technology arm of Compass Group. Chris works with a talented team across digital strategy, UX research & design, Product Development, and marketing for the Education and B&I sectors. The Food Services Digital team is focused on creating the future of digital hospitality through products, operational processes, and customer models to enhance guest experience while increasing operational efficiency and insight. Prior to Compass Digital, Chris was an Executive Chef in Charleston, SC for 8 years, a Regional Chef with Compass Group for 7 years, and has moved through the Compass Digital organization initially in digital strategy and expanding to product, ux, and marketing functions. He leverages his culinary and operational background to influence digital transformation with an end user perspective. Along with 20 years of culinary experience, Chris also holds a bachelor’s degree in IT with a focus on User Experience. He resides in Charleston, SC with his wife Alicia and three boys, PJ, Logan, and Griffin.

speaker_job_title: Managing Director, US Retail & Consumer Goods
speaker_description: Maggie Lyons leads a team of sales, technical and domain experts working to solve the most challenging data problems for SAS retail and consumer goods customers. Lyons brings over 15 years of experience working in Washington on federal policy initiatives. She joined SAS most recently from the US Department of Agriculture, where she worked closely with consumer goods companies and led the oversight of federal programs delivered to consumers via food retailers.

Harvey Ma is a 20-year executive with a background in leading international teams across businesses in retail, manufacturing, engineering and teleco. He is SVP of Retail, NielsenIQ, leading overall portfolio strategy focused on delivering both industry and consumer intelligence products to retailers. Prior, he was Head of Sales & Marketing at Roundel, the in-house marketing/ad agency for Target Corporation. Ma has a Master’s in BA from UMN and a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from UT, Austin.
Katishi Maake is a writer for Retail Brew based out of New York City. He joined Morning Brew in January 2021.

speaker_job_title: Chief Technology and Supply Chain Officer

speaker_description: Satish Malhotra is Chief Executive Officer and President of The Container Store, the nation’s leading specialty retailer of organizing solutions, custom spaces and in-home services. He joined the company in February 2021 after 21 years in various leadership positions at prestige beauty retailer Sephora Americas.

Amitabh Mall was appointed Chief Analytics Officer in July 2021 and leads the advanced analytics agenda for Woolworths Group. In this role Amitabh is responsible for leveraging the power of data and analytics to help our teams and partners serve our customers better. He is concurrently also the Managing Director of wiq where he is building a globally leading retail analytics capability by combining the best of Quantium data science and product capabilities with the retail experience in Woolworths Group.Prior to this, Amitabh was a Senior Partner & Managing Director at Boston Consulting Group (BCG) where he most recently led their Consumer and Retail practice in Asia-Pacific. In a career spanning 20 years at BCG, Amitabh advised leading retailers and consumer companies across Australia, North America and Asia. He has worked extensively in the area of transformation, powered by digital and analytics capabilities.

Jill Manoff is the editor-in-chief of Glossy, which explores the transformation of the fashion, luxury, beauty and retail industries through the lens of digital and technology. She oversees the editorial content and growth strategy of the media brand, launched in May 2016. Manoff joined Glossy from Mode Media, where she was the editor of Glam.com starting in July 2015. Prior to that, she was the editor of searsStyle and head copywriter in Sears’ apparel division. She spent the start of her career in St. Louis as the fashion editor of Alive magazine, the fashion director and co-founder of Saint Louis Fashion Week, and a freelance fashion stylist, working for brands including Anheuser-Busch and Caleres. Glossy is a media brand exploring how fashion, luxury and beauty industries are being disrupted by digital media and technology. Launched in 2016, it examines how technology is upending established brands and retailers, and giving rise to newcomers in these spaces. The Glossy platform reaches its audience through editorial content, emails and the Glossy Podcast, as well as in-person gatherings including summits, one-day forums and awards galas.

Judd Marcello is the Chief Marketing Officer of FarEye, responsible for all facets of marketing, including brand strategy, demand generation, and product marketing. Judd is a career marketer and accomplished software executive with global experience helping growth stage & mature SaaS businesses scale brand awareness and pipeline generation. Judd has delivered high-impact B2C and B2B marketing leadership in the software, CPG, consumer electronics, and F&B industries within the United Kingdom, Americas, Australia, Asia-Pacific, and European markets.

speaker_job_title: VP & GM Artificial Intelligence for Retail and CPG
speaker_description: Azita Martin leads the NVIDIA retail team. She's responsible for global go-to-market strategy for the vertical. She advises senior executives on best practices of leveraging AI to improve operational efficiencies and identify new revenue streams. Previously, Azita was the chief marketing officer at Maana, an AI software platform used by industrial companies to accelerate building AI applications. Azita has been working with the chief digital officers and heads of analytics/AI of Fortune 500 industrial companies, and is intimately familiar with the top AI use-cases that are demonstrating business value.

Trevor Martin leads Syndigo’s Retail Client Development practice, with dual responsibility for client account teams and program management for retail/recipient clients. Before joining Syndigo, Trevor spent nine years at Numerator, working with large retailer, distributor, and brand manufacturers across multiple industry verticals, providing competitive intelligence in pricing, promotions, and advertising. Previously, Trevor spent nearly a decade at Corporate Executive Board, with roles including executive education, account management, and IT project management.

Faisal Masud is the Chief Executive Officer of fabric, an-industry-leading modular and scalable commerce platform to empower retailers to accelerate growth across all channels. With more than 20 years building and operating large-scale ecommerce businesses at Google, Amazon, eBay, Groupon, and Staples, Faisal's experience is centered around managing P&Ls and transforming experiences on behalf of the customer. Faisal's focus areas are merchandising, last-mile delivery, third-party marketplaces, and customer-facing product platforms.

Coleen Matsuo is Senior Director of Technology for The Home Depot.  She is responsible for the strategy, architecture and engineering of enterprise data, cloud networks as well as unified communications and enterprise monitoring/observability. Coleen has led her team in the renewal and expansion of The Home Depot’s data network environment including expansion to hybrid cloud, SD-WAN deployment, introduction of software defined networking components across two data centers and 2200 retail location and configuration automation.

Brian McGlynn is General Manager of Commerce at Coveo. Having spent more than 20 years in the high-tech industry, Brian’s experience spans start-ups and Fortune 100 companies, including HP and IBM. He’s held roles in sales, consulting, and general management. More recently as General Manager of Intershop’s North American business, Brian spearheaded the company’s growth into new markets with B2C and B2B commerce offerings.

speaker_job_title: Chairman of the Board and CEO
speaker_description: Rodney currently serves as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of The Kroger Co. He joined Kroger in 1978 as a part-time stock clerk in Lexington, KY. During his career with Kroger, he has served in numerous leadership positions, including Assistant Treasurer, Vice President of Planning and Capital Management, Corporate Controller, Chief Financial Officer, Senior Vice President, Executive Vice President and Vice Chairman. He was elected to Kroger’s Board of Directors in 2003, to President and Chief Operating Officer in 2009, and to his current position in 2014. Rodney was named Chairman of the Board in 2015.Rodney is a member of the Board of Directors of Cincinnati Financial Corp., VF Corporation, Cincinnati Center City Development Corporation (3CDC), Cincinnati Business Committee and Consumer Goods Forum. He also serves on the Board of Trustees of Xavier University and on the Dean’s Advisory Council of University of Kentucky Gatton College of Business and Economics. Rodney earned bachelor’s degrees in Accounting and Finance, and an MBA with an Accounting concentration, all from the University of Kentucky. He and his wife, Kathy, live in Cincinnati and enjoy traveling and hiking.

James McQuivey, author of the book Digital Disruption, tracks and predicts behavior change among consumers and employees. He focuses on understanding core human needs and motivations to determine why, when, and how people will try new things. He is the research director for Forrester's Future of Work team, guiding analysts to identify changes in the world of work and prepare clients in every industry to approach these changes as opportunities instead of threats.

Christopher McShane is the SVP of Customer Experience at Serta Simmons Bedding. Prior to SSB, he held roles in sales, program management, and operations across different industries. Chris holds an MBA from Emory University and is an Air Force veteran. He lives in Atlanta, GA with his wife and 2 daughters.

Áine McTiernan is the Head of Customer Success for Peak, a Decision Intelligence company based in New York City. Within this role, Áine is responsible for making sure Peak delivers the best possible business outcomes and collaborative experience to our customers, including brands such as Nike, Molson Coors and KFC. Áine works across the Retail, CPG and Manufacturing verticals. In her free time, she enjoys reading, great wine and taking walks with her puppy, Socks!

Charlie McWeeney serves as Vice President of Technology, Innovation, and Strategy at Wakefern Food Corp., the largest retailer-owned cooperative in the United States, comprising 50 member companies who independently own and operate 362 retail supermarkets under the ShopRite, Price Rite Marketplace, The Fresh Grocer, Dearborn Market, Gourmet Garage, and Fairway Market banners in New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, and Rhode Island.

speaker_company: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton Inc.
speaker_description: Anish Melwani is the Chairman & CEO, LVMH for North America. In this role, he oversees and coordinates the activities of the LVMH Group across 75+ Maisons.  He sits on the board of directors for Fresh, Marc Jacobs, Colgin Cellars, Starboard Cruise Services and Tiffany & Co.  Prior to joining LVMH in 2015, he was a Senior Partner in the New York Office of McKinsey and Company where he co-led the Global Strategy & Corporate Finance practice and supported clients across industries. At McKinsey since 1999, he counseled senior executives of leading global companies on issues related to corporate strategy, M&A, alliances, portfolio management and organization. He worked in McKinsey’s Singapore and Hong Kong offices and was then relocated to the New York office, where he advised leaders of public sector institutions in New York City.Mr. Melwani is a member of the Council on Foreign Relations, the Board of the United Way of New York City where he serves on the Marketing Committee, and the National Retail Federation’s Board of Directors. He holds a BA in Economics from Harvard University.

John Miller is Chairman & CEO of Cali Group.  Prior to founding Cali Group in 2011, he was the second employee at Arrowhead (NASDAQ: ARWR) where he was responsible for formation, growth, and sale of Arrowhead’s electronics business unit.  John graduated Order of the Coif from Stanford Law School and is an author of The Handbook of Nanotechnology Business, Policy, and Intellectual Property Law as well as various other publications related to nanomaterials and nanoscale electronics.

Kevin Miller, Chief Marketing Officer of The Fresh Market, was named as one of the 30 Most Influential Marketers on LinkedIn in 2022 and received the 2021 CMO Innovator of the Year Award from the Millennium Alliance. Kevin assumed The Fresh Market CMO position in May 2020. In just over two years, he has helped lead The Fresh Market to eight straight quarters of comparable sales growth despite significant economic, supply chain and inflationary headwinds.

Hilary Milnes is the executive Americas editor at Vogue Business, where she oversees coverage on the fashion, beauty and luxury businesses. Before joining Vogue Business, she was editor of Modern Retail, a digital trade publication covering the changes happening in the retail industry. Prior, she helped launch Glossy, an online fashion trade, and served as its managing editor until 2018.
